text.heading: On Osun Law School students’ bursary
text.p: SIR: Payment of bursary to students in the Nigerian Law School is an important incentive which enhances the academic performance of the students. This is because the expenses of an average student whilst at the Law School is quite high. Apart from the school fee which is N220,000, a law student still has to buy text books, Laptop and cater for his feeding amongst other expenses.

Fortunately for some Nigerian Law School students, some State Governments have made it a policy to give a particular sum of money as bursary to the students from their states. Such states include but are not limited to the following:
(1) Kaduna State paid 250,000(Two Hundred and Fifty Thousand) Naira per Law Student.
(2) Lagos State paid 200,000 (Two Hundred Thousand) Naira per Law Student.
(3) Ekiti State paid 100,000 (One Hundred Thousand) Naira per Law Student.
(4) Ogun State paid 50,000 (Fifty Thousand) Naira per Law Student.
(5) Kogi State paid 150,000 (One Hundred and Fifty Thousand) Naira per Law Student.
(6) Kwara State paid 200,000 (Two Hundred Thousand) Naira per Law Student.
However, despite several entreaties and appeals made to the Osun State Government, Governor Olagunsoye Oyinlola has refused to pay Osun State students in the Nigerian Law School any form of bursary.

It is pertinent to note that Governor Oyinlola is a law graduate from the Buckingham University in the United Kingdom; his Deputy, Erelu Olusola Obada, is a lawyer whilst the Speaker of the Osun State House of Assembly, Hon. Adejare Bello, is also a lawyer.

Further, Oyinlola’s argument that if he pays bursary to law students, students from other disciplines would demand for same, is dead on arrival. This is because the legal profession is the ONLY profession which demands that a student must attend a post-university school (The Nigerian Law School) before he can be called to the Nigerian Bar.
I hereby use this medium to implore Governor Oyinlola to include the payment of bursary to Law School students from Osun in the state’s budget.
